seoTitle: Upskill in Operations & Support — skills, next roles & moves | SkillKite
seoDescription: Upskill in Operations & Support — process, tools, next roles & side moves

guide.heading: SkillKite — Next career rung for you
guide.greeting: Hey there! You're currently working in Operations & Support, and you're still figuring out where you want to take things next — that's completely fine. Below is a clear-eyed breakdown of the highest-leverage skills, realistic next roles, and adjacent paths that professionals in your position typically benefit from most. Take what fits your situation.

sections.title: Skills to add now
sections.intro: These skills have strong salary and seniority leverage for ops/support professionals — whether you stay in the same domain or eventually pivot.

options.name: Process Automation with Python or Power Automate
options.whatIsIt: Using Python scripts or Microsoft Power Automate to automate repetitive manual tasks — report generation, data entry, email triggers, escalation workflows. No deep coding needed; workflow-level scripting is enough.
options.whoFor: Operations executives and support leads who spend hours on repetitive tasks and want to show visible business impact without becoming full developers.
options.leadsTo: Process Improvement Analyst, Automation Specialist, RPA roles — salary bump from ₹25k-40k to ₹50k-90k in mid-market; remote roles at ₹70k-1.2L are common.
options.keyExams: Microsoft Power Automate Associate (PL-900 + PL-500), or Python for Automation on Coursera/LinkedIn Learning (no formal cert needed, portfolio matters more).
options.timeCommitment: 2-3 months to get job-ready with 2-3 real automation projects documented.

options.name: Data Analysis with Excel (Advanced) + Power BI or Tableau
options.whatIsIt: Advanced Excel means pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, Power Query, and dashboards. Power BI or Tableau adds interactive reporting that managers and clients actually act on. Together they turn raw ops data into decisions.
options.whoFor: Anyone in ops/support who already handles MIS reports, SLA tracking, or daily metrics — this formalises and upgrades what you already do.
options.leadsTo: MIS Analyst, Operations Analyst, Business Analyst roles — ₹40k-80k in Tier 2/3 cities, ₹70k-1.2L for remote BPO/ITES roles. Very high demand.
options.keyExams: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst (PL-300) — well-recognized and reasonably achievable. Tableau Desktop Specialist is an alternative.
options.timeCommitment: 2-3 months for Excel + Power BI together if you practice on real data.

options.name: ITIL 4 Foundation (Service Management Framework)
options.whatIsIt: ITIL 4 is the global standard framework for IT service management — incident management, change management, SLA design, service desk governance. It gives ops/support professionals a structured language that corporate and MNC hiring managers recognize instantly.
options.whoFor: Support engineers, service desk leads, IT ops professionals, and anyone managing tickets or SLAs who wants a formal credential to back up what they already do daily.
options.leadsTo: IT Service Manager, Service Desk Manager, ITSM Consultant — ₹50k-1L in India; strong in MNCs, GCCs, and BPOs. The cert alone can unlock a ₹10k-20k increment in many organizations.
options.keyExams: ITIL 4 Foundation — globally recognized, costs roughly ₹8k-15k with exam, achievable in 4-6 weeks of focused study.
options.timeCommitment: 1-2 months to clear the exam; immediate resume impact.

options.name: Project Management Fundamentals (PMP or Google PM Certificate)
options.whatIsIt: Structured project management — scope definition, stakeholder communication, risk tracking, delivery timelines using tools like Jira, Asana, or MS Project. Google's PM certificate covers agile and waterfall both in a digestible format.
options.whoFor: Ops professionals who already coordinate between teams, manage vendor SLAs, or handle cross-functional tasks — this formalizes that leadership and makes you a viable PM candidate.
options.leadsTo: Project Coordinator, Operations Project Manager, Program Analyst — ₹45k-90k in Tier 2/3; remote coordinator roles at ₹60k-1.2L. PMP unlocks senior PM at ₹1L+.
options.keyExams: Google Project Management Certificate (Coursera, ~6 months, affordable) as entry point; PMP (PMI) for senior roles — requires 3 years experience, costs ~₹25k-35k all-in.
options.timeCommitment: 3-4 months for Google cert; 6-8 months for PMP prep if you have the experience eligibility.

options.name: Customer Success Management (CSM) Principles
options.whatIsIt: Customer Success is about proactively ensuring clients derive ongoing value from a product or service — onboarding, health scoring, renewal conversations, escalation handling. It is the strategic upgrade from reactive support to revenue-impacting relationship management.
options.whoFor: Support professionals with 2+ years of client interaction experience who want to move away from ticket queues into a higher-value, higher-pay client-facing role.
options.leadsTo: Customer Success Manager, Account Manager (SaaS), Client Onboarding Specialist — ₹50k-1L in Indian SaaS companies; high demand in Bangalore, Pune, Hyderabad remote roles too.
options.keyExams: SuccessHACKER / Gainsight Customer Success Certification, or HubSpot Customer Success certification (free). Portfolio of documented client impact cases matters more than the cert.
options.timeCommitment: 2-3 months to learn + build case studies; transition itself takes 3-6 months of targeted job applications.

options.name: SQL for Operations Data
options.whatIsIt: Structured Query Language — writing queries to pull, filter, join, and summarize data from databases. In ops/support contexts this means extracting ticket data, SLA reports, workforce metrics, and customer data without waiting for a developer.
options.whoFor: Operations analysts, support leads, and MIS professionals who rely on data but currently depend on other teams to pull reports. Even basic SQL dramatically increases your independence and perceived value.
options.leadsTo: Operations Analyst, Data Analyst (Operations), Business Intelligence Associate — ₹40k-80k mid-level; pairing SQL with Power BI can push you to ₹80k-1.2L remote roles.
options.keyExams: No formal cert needed — HackerRank SQL certification (free, recognized) or Google Data Analytics Certificate (Coursera) covers SQL as part of a broader curriculum.
options.timeCommitment: 1.5-2 months to reach job-ready SQL proficiency with daily practice.

sections.title: Roles to target next
sections.intro: These are realistic next-rung roles for ops/support professionals with 1-10 years of experience — ranked by accessibility and market demand.

options.name: Operations Analyst / MIS Analyst
options.whatIsIt: Day-to-day work involves tracking operational KPIs, building dashboards, preparing performance reports, identifying inefficiencies, and presenting data-backed recommendations to management. Heavy Excel/Power BI use, some SQL.
options.whoFor: Ops/support professionals who already handle any form of reporting, data reconciliation, or metrics tracking and want to make that their core job with a proper title and pay.
options.leadsTo: ₹35k-65k in Tier 2/3 cities; ₹60k-1L for remote roles in ITES/BPO/SaaS companies. Senior MIS/Ops Analyst roles at ₹80k-1.2L are achievable within 2-3 years.
options.keyExams: PL-300 (Power BI) strengthens the profile; not mandatory but helpful for MNC hiring.
options.timeCommitment: 3-5 months of skill-building before a realistic job switch.

options.name: IT Service Desk Manager / ITSM Lead
options.whatIsIt: Manages a team of service desk agents, owns SLA performance, handles escalation frameworks, vendor coordination, and process improvement. Uses tools like ServiceNow, Freshservice, or Zendesk at a governance level.
options.whoFor: Support professionals with 3+ years of experience who have informally been playing a team lead or quality checker role and want that formalized with a management title.
options.leadsTo: ₹50k-90k in Tier 2/3; ₹80k-1.3L in GCCs and MNCs. ITIL 4 certification significantly accelerates hiring at this level.
options.keyExams: ITIL 4 Foundation — near-mandatory for this role in organized companies.
options.timeCommitment: 2-4 months with ITIL prep; lateral move if you already have team lead experience.

options.name: Customer Success Manager (CSM)
options.whatIsIt: Owns a portfolio of clients, ensures product adoption, conducts business reviews, handles renewals, and flags churn risks. Works closely with product, sales, and support teams. Very communication-heavy, relationship-driven role.
options.whoFor: Support professionals with strong client communication experience, especially in SaaS, software, or B2B service companies. Suits people who enjoy problem-solving conversations more than ticket resolution.
options.leadsTo: ₹45k-90k in Indian SaaS; ₹80k-1.5L for enterprise CSM roles in larger companies. High growth ceiling — Senior CSM and VP Customer Success are well-paying tracks.
options.keyExams: No hard gate, but Gainsight or HubSpot CSM certifications improve credibility. Strong portfolio of client case studies matters most.
options.timeCommitment: 3-6 months of upskilling + targeted job applications; easier transition if you're already in a SaaS-adjacent company.

options.name: Project Coordinator / Junior Project Manager
options.whatIsIt: Tracks project timelines, manages task assignments in Jira or Asana, prepares status updates, coordinates between technical and business teams, and flags risks. Stepping stone to full Project Manager title.
options.whoFor: Ops professionals who naturally become the go-to person for coordinating multi-team work, chasing deadlines, and keeping people aligned. Suits organized, communication-strong individuals.
options.leadsTo: ₹35k-65k as coordinator; ₹60k-1.1L as Project Manager with PMP or 3-4 years experience. Remote PM roles for US/UK clients pay ₹80k-1.5L.
options.keyExams: Google PM Certificate for entry; PMP for senior roles.
options.timeCommitment: 3-5 months to skill up; transition timeline depends on current company — internal moves are often faster.

options.name: Process Improvement / RPA Analyst
options.whatIsIt: Identifies manual, repetitive processes in operations and designs automation solutions using tools like UiPath, Automation Anywhere, or Power Automate. Documents workflows, calculates ROI of automation, and works with developers or builds bots directly.
options.whoFor: Ops professionals with process knowledge who want to move into a higher-paying, more technical adjacent role without becoming a full software developer. Great fit for detail-oriented people who understand end-to-end workflows.
options.leadsTo: ₹50k-1L in mid-sized companies and GCCs; ₹80k-1.4L for senior RPA roles. One of the fastest-growing ops-adjacent specializations in India right now.
options.keyExams: UiPath RPA Developer Foundation (free, recognized) or Automation Anywhere Certified Advanced RPA Professional.
options.timeCommitment: 3-5 months to get RPA tool-ready and build 2-3 automation portfolio projects.

sections.title: Side moves
sections.intro: Adjacent fields where your ops/support experience transfers well — useful to know even if you're not planning a switch right now.

options.name: Business Analysis (BA)
options.whatIsIt: Business Analysts bridge the gap between business operations and technology by documenting requirements, mapping processes, and ensuring solutions actually solve real problems. Your ops experience gives you a head start on understanding real workflows.
options.whoFor: Ops professionals who understand how processes break down, have communicated with both business stakeholders and technical teams, and enjoy structured problem-solving over execution.
options.leadsTo: Junior BA roles at ₹45k-80k; mid-level BA at ₹80k-1.4L. High demand in IT services, GCCs, banking, and product companies. Good abroad options too.
options.keyExams: IIBA ECBA (Entry Certificate in Business Analysis) — globally recognized, good starting credential. CBAP for senior level.
options.timeCommitment: 3-5 months to learn BA tools (JIRA, Confluence, BPMN, use case writing) and prep for ECBA.

options.name: Human Resources Operations (HR Ops / HR Analytics)
options.whatIsIt: HR Operations covers payroll coordination, HRMS tool management, compliance tracking, onboarding workflows, and workforce data reporting. HR Analytics adds a data layer — headcount analysis, attrition modelling, hiring funnel metrics.
options.whoFor: Ops support professionals who have worked with HR systems, payroll vendors, or employee data in any capacity. Especially relevant for those in BPOs, staffing firms, or large shared services setups.
options.leadsTo: HR Ops Executive to HR Ops Manager track — ₹35k-70k; HR Analytics roles pay ₹50k-1L. Steady demand across all industries.
options.keyExams: No hard gate cert; SHRM-CP is internationally recognized if you want to go premium. Excel/Power BI skills matter more here.
options.timeCommitment: 2-3 months to bridge skill gaps if you already have ops exposure; internal lateral moves are often possible.

options.name: Sales Operations / Revenue Operations (RevOps)
options.whatIsIt: Sales Ops professionals manage CRM data (Salesforce, HubSpot), build sales dashboards, track pipeline health, design commission structures, and support the sales team with process and data. RevOps broadens this across marketing and customer success.
options.whoFor: Ops professionals who have worked in or around sales, customer-facing, or B2B environments and have any exposure to CRM tools or sales reporting. Strong Excel/SQL skills accelerate entry.
options.leadsTo: Sales Ops Analyst at ₹45k-85k; Senior Sales Ops / RevOps Manager at ₹90k-1.6L. High-paying track in SaaS, fintech, and e-commerce companies.
options.keyExams: Salesforce Administrator certification (Salesforce Admin 201) — well-recognized and commands a salary premium. HubSpot Operations Hub cert (free) as a starter.
options.timeCommitment: 3-4 months for Salesforce Admin cert + CRM tool practice; transition takes 3-6 months of targeted applications.

guide.closingMessage: You're in a field where the right skill additions can genuinely double your salary within 18-24 months — ops/support professionals who pick up data, automation, or service management skills are in very high demand right now. You don't need to have your destination figured out yet. Pick one skill from the list above that feels closest to your current work, spend 2-3 months getting genuinely good at it, and the direction will start to become clearer. Come back to SkillKite when you're ready for a specific role-level roadmap. Share this with a colleague who's in the same boat — they'll thank you for it. 🪁
